close_reason: Not a bug: inside spawn_blocking, Handle::try_current() returns Ok — there is no API to detect the blocking thread pool. The scoped thread approach is correct and safe. Overhead is negligible vs HSM I/O.
description: sign_sync detects 'are we inside a tokio runtime?' via Handle::try_current(). When called from spawn_blocking (the expected use pattern for wrapping async code in sync APIs), try_current() succeeds and the code correctly spawns a scoped thread. However the docs comment only 'safe from any calling context' without explaining the spawn_blocking interaction. More critically: each try_sign call in the no-runtime path creates and destroys a full tokio runtime. For KMS backends this prevents connection reuse across calls since the HTTP client (and its connection pool) is torn down with the runtime. If the backend holds its HTTP client internally this is fine; if it relies on the tokio runtime for connection keepalive, each sign pays full TLS handshake cost.
title: sign_sync spawns thread even inside spawn_blocking
